summary: DHH highlights Kathy Sierra's 'Creating Passionate Users' blog as essential reading, noting her coverage of Ruby on Rails and 37signals. He uses the post itself as a meta-demonstration of how passion spreads virally between creators and audiences. The core message is that Rails' explosive growth stems not from technical superiority alone but from cultivating genuine user passion. He points to Sierra's framework of making users 'kick ass' as the explanation for why some projects generate massive momentum while others don't.
key_points: Kathy Sierra's 'Creating Passionate Users' framework explains why Ruby on Rails was generating outsized attention
Success comes from making users feel empowered and passionate, not just from technical features
Passion spreads virally through mutual enthusiasm — creators amplifying each other creates a positive feedback loop
The post itself is a live demonstration of the passion-spreading dynamic it describes
money_quotes: I keep getting questions from people who wants to know why it is that Ruby on Rails are making 10-meter waves at the moment.
It's all about the passion, about making users "kick ass", and for getting that Next Level groove going.

See how it just happened in this post? I really liked Kathy's work, she blogged about mine, I returned the ball, and know you are probably positioned in an even more positive place about both of our stuff.
key_insight: The explosive growth of a technology project is driven not by features but by cultivating genuine passion that spreads virally between creators and users.
